checks.description: Do not infer business-level completion or acceptance solely from reset activity.
notes: Clock/reset remain anchors and are not emitted as business outputs.
checks.pass_condition: No safety property is violated by treating reset as control-only; later functional claims require separate post-reset evidence.
checks.trigger_condition: While reset is asserted or being released.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'reset_001' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: avoid fixed-cycle expectations unless the contract later proves them.
notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires conservative, externally visible progress or stability and forbids hidden exact-cycle assumptions.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

checks.description: Guardrail: avoid fixed-cycle expectations unless the contract later proves them.
notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires conservative, externally visible progress or stability and forbids hidden exact-cycle assumptions.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'edge_001' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: acceptance requires legal valid/ready overlap.
notes: Prevents false positives from incorrectly inferring transaction acceptance.
checks.pass_condition: Acceptance is only credited on legal handshake overlap and is never inferred from a later output alone.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the case tries to interpret a transfer as accepted.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'protocol_001' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: do not declare acceptance or completion while backpressure is active.
notes: Property oracle is ambiguity-aware and avoids overcommitting to stall behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The design is only required to avoid premature acceptance/completion claims; it is not required to make progress under backpressure.
checks.trigger_condition: When ready is low during an attempted transfer.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'protocol_002' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: do not declare completion before a legal acceptance event becomes visible.
notes: Safety-style property used because persistence semantics are not fully proven.
checks.pass_condition: Completion or success is not credited before a later legal acceptance or progress event is externally visible.
checks.trigger_condition: While a source is waiting for the protocol to permit acceptance.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'protocol_003' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: repeated operations must not collapse into a single ambiguous completion claim.
notes: Stability-style property used instead of throughput-specific claims.
checks.pass_condition: Observed progress remains order-aware and non-contradictory even when exact queueing semantics are unresolved.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked case applies repeated or back-to-back operations.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'back_to_back_001' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Treat reset assertion/release as a control event and require reset-safe interface behavior.
notes: Reset is used as an anchor only; it is not treated as a business output.
checks.pass_condition: No acceptance, completion, or externally visible functional progress is claimed solely during reset; post-release observation waits for stable, externally visible behavior.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset 'rst' is asserted and later released according to the inferred polarity.

notes: Reset oracle focuses on control-safe behavior instead of value-specific output claims.
Protocol oracle strictness is downgraded because the contract or plan is weak.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Recognize acceptance only when valid and ready are simultaneously asserted.
notes: Acceptance-safe oracle; not a completion oracle.
checks.pass_condition: A transaction may be considered accepted only on valid/ready overlap; downstream completion is not required in the same observation point or any predetermined later observation point.
checks.trigger_condition: When 'res_valid' and 'res_ready' overlap in the observation window.

notes: Protocol oracle strictness is downgraded because the contract or plan is weak.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Guard against claiming acceptance or completion while ready is low.
checks.pass_condition: Progress may stall or be deferred; the oracle does not require completion or acceptance until a later legal handshake is observed.
checks.trigger_condition: When 'res_valid' is asserted while 'res_ready' remains low or unavailable for acceptance.

notes: Protocol oracle strictness is downgraded because the contract or plan is weak.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Observe source-side persistence or other safe waiting behavior before acceptance.
notes: Persistence semantics remain heuristic and are intentionally checked in a safety-style form.
checks.pass_condition: The interface remains externally consistent while waiting; completion is not claimed before a legal acceptance event is visible.
checks.trigger_condition: When 'res_valid' stays asserted across multiple conservative observation opportunities before 'res_ready' allows acceptance.

notes: Protocol oracle strictness is downgraded because the contract or plan is weak.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Require repeated operations to be distinguished by separate protocol-visible acceptance or completion events.
notes: Repeated-operation oracle avoids throughput and queue-depth assumptions.
checks.pass_condition: A second operation is not treated as accepted or complete unless a distinct later protocol-visible event is observed.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked plan applies repeated or back-to-back operations with minimal spacing.

notes: Protocol oracle strictness is downgraded because the contract or plan is weak.
